question: What came to Joel son of Pethuel (Joel 1:1)?

Why does Joel tell the drinkers of wine to wail (Joel 1:5)?
options: Because the locusts have eaten your lunch
Because the vines have been devoured
Because the new wine has been snatched from your lips

question: How have the locusts attached the fig trees (Joel 1:7)?
options: Destroyed all the figs both ripe and unripe
Stripped off their bark leaving the branches white
Devoured the tree down to its roots

Those who carry the ark of the covenant

question: What two types of offerings does Joel have the priests grieve over (Joel 1:13)?

What are broken down (Joel 1:17)?

options: The fences have been broken down

question: What has devoured the open pastures (Joel 1:19)?
Who does Joel say pants for God (Joel 1:20)?
Why does God say all in the land should tremble (Joel 2:1)?
options: Because the fields will be burned with fire
Because the Feast of Trumpets is at hand
Because the Day of the Lord is coming close

question: What is Israel to do because the day of the Lord is coming close at hand (Joel 2:1)?
options: Sound the alarm by blowing the trumpet
Close the city gates and lock them with bars
Put on sackcloth and put dust on their heads

question: What goes before the large and mighty army (Joel 2:3)?

options: The green banks of the Jordan River

question: What does the large and mighty army leave behind it (Joel 2:3)?
How does each soldier in the large and mighty army march (Joel 2:8)?
options: With the speed of a leopard

question: What does the large and mighty army do without breaking ranks (Joel 2:8)?
What does the large and mighty army climb into (Joel 2:9)?

Over the thresholds like a tax collector

Of what does God relent (Joel 2:13)?
What does Joel say should be done in Zion (Joel 2:15)?
options: Dust should be put on their heads

question: What does Joel suggest should be declared (Joel 2:15)?

Has there ever been such devastation?
Why has the Lord rejected his people?

question: When will the Lord take pity on his people (Joel 2:18)?
options: When he has compassion on them
When he is jealous for his land

question: Where the front columns of the northern army go (Joel 2:20)?
What has God given in righteousness (Joel 2:23)?

What will their vats overflow with (Joel 2:24)?

Because he has worked his wonders for them
Because he has sent rain in its season
Because the locusts were burned alive

question: What will never happen to Israel again (Joel 2:26)?
options: They will never see another locust plague
They will never experience hunger again
They will never be shamed again
They will never return to Egypt

The prophets of the Most High
Those who follow the way of the Lord

options: It will shine even in the day
It will be turned to blood

question: When will the sun be turned to darkness (Joel 2:31)?
options: Before the coming great day of the Lord
Before the coming of the One to whom it belongs

Before the scepter of Israel rules over all nations

question: Who will be saved in those days (Joel 2:32)?

All who call on the name of the Lord
All who turn to the Lord
All who turn away from gods of silver and gold

question: Where will deliverance be in those days (Joel 2:32)?

The fortunes of Judah and Jerusalem
The borders of the land of Israel and Judah

question: Where are multitudes (Joel 3:14)?
